statusLine: European fuel security remains under strain with 17 of 27 countries holding critical reserves in at least one fuel type, though overall EU averages stay above mandatory minimums.
fullAnalysis:
Despite EU-wide averages meeting the 90-day mandatory minimum (petrol 106.8 days, diesel 99 days, jet fuel 93.7 days), fuel security across Europe remains under significant pressure. A majority of member states—17 of 27 countries—are experiencing critical shortages in at least one fuel category, with Romania, Poland, and Luxembourg facing the most severe challenges across all three fuel types. Romania's situation is particularly concerning, with petrol and diesel reserves at just 33.2 and 32.4 days respectively, well below the 90-day threshold.

Jet fuel presents the most widespread vulnerability, with 19 of 27 countries holding critical or warning-level reserves. This shortage pattern affects major aviation hubs including Germany (64.7 days), France (59.6 days), and the Netherlands (59 days), potentially constraining air transport capacity. Diesel shortages are also notable, with 14 countries below safe levels, including key economies like Germany (64.3 days) and Spain (68.6 days).

Fuel prices reflect ongoing supply tensions, with Brent crude at $108.29/barrel—up 49.4% since late February's Middle East escalation. The €1.78 average petrol price shows significant variation across the bloc, from Malta's €1.34 to the Netherlands' €2.32, indicating different national taxation policies and supply chain pressures. While recent strategic reserve releases have helped maintain overall EU averages above critical thresholds, the uneven distribution of reserves across member states highlights persistent supply chain vulnerabilities.
keyPoints: 17 of 27 EU countries face critical reserves in at least one fuel type, despite overall EU averages remaining above 90-day minimums
Romania shows the most severe shortages with petrol at 33.2 days and diesel at 32.4 days—both far below the mandatory 90-day threshold
Jet fuel presents the broadest vulnerability with 19 of 27 countries at critical or warning levels, affecting major aviation hubs including Germany and France
Oil prices have surged 49.4% since late February's Middle East escalation, with Brent crude now at $108.29/barrel
Diesel shortages affect 14 countries including major economies Germany and Spain, while petrol shortages are concentrated in 12 member states
